授業目的: The objective of the course is to understand the nature of international trade. It covers the basic methods to understand issues raised from the economic interaction between countries. Why international trade arises and what effects it has on the countries involved. Then based on the perspective of international trade, we will look at the economic development in the Asia-Pacific region.
到達目標: After taking this course, students should i) have learned the basic concepts of international trade theory, ii) understand the trade issues of economies in the Asia-Pacific region.

トピック.授業外学習2: There is no prerequisites for this course. However, for the further study, it will be good for student to take or learn introductory economics in advance.

参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
1.	Krugman, Paul R. and Maurice Obstfeld. (2014). International Economics: Theory and Policy (10th ed.). Boston, MA: Pearson Addison-Wesley. 
2.	McLaren, John. (2013). International Trade. Hoboken, NJ: John Wiley & Sons.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school.By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.North American Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
